scenes.content:
Title:
	_**BRICK & STEEL**_
	_**FULL RETIRED**_
Credit: Written by
Author: Stu Maschwitz
Source: Story by KTM
Draft date: 1/27/2012
Contact:
	Next Level Productions
	1588 Mission Dr.
	Solvang, CA 93463

A gorgeous day.  The sun is shining.  But BRICK BRADDOCK, retired police detective, is sitting quietly, contemplating -- something.

The SCREEN DOOR slides open and DICK STEEL, his former partner and fellow retiree, emerges with two cold beers.

STEEL
Beer's ready!

BRICK
Are they cold?

STEEL
Does a bear crap in the woods?

Steel sits.  They laugh at the dumb joke.

STEEL
(beer raised)
To retirement.

BRICK
To retirement.

They drink long and well from the beers.

And then there's a long beat.
Longer than is funny.
Long enough to be depressing.

The men look at each other.

STEEL
Screw retirement.

BRICK ^
Screw retirement.

SMASH CUT TO:

dialogues.text: Does a bear crap in the woods?

scenes.summary: On Brick's patio on a gorgeous day, the sun shines brightly as retired detective Brick Braddock sits lost in thought. His former partner and fellow retiree, Dick Steel, emerges with two cold beers, breaking the silence with a lighthearted joke that briefly lifts the tension. As they laugh and toast to retirement, their camaraderie appears genuine, but a long, uncomfortable pause follows, hinting at deeper issues. The men exchange a weighted glance, and in a shocking reversal, Steel's words and Brick's mirror response shift from celebratory to defiant, abandoning their earlier enthusiasm for the idea of retirement, leaving an air of uncertainty and foreboding.

scenes.content:
This is the home of THE BOY BAND, AKA DAN and JACK.  They too are drinking beer, and counting the take from their last smash-and-grab.  Money, drugs, and ridiculous props are strewn about the table.

			JACK
		(in Vietnamese, subtitled)
	*Did you know Brick and Steel are retired?*

			DAN
	Then let's retire them.
	_Permanently_.

Jack begins to argue vociferously in Vietnamese (?), But mercifully we...

				CUT TO:

dialogues.text: *Did you know Brick and Steel are retired?*

scenes.summary: As the Boy Band's trailer home fills with the smell of stale beer and discarded revelry, a tension-fueled conversation unfolds between Dan and Jack over the table littered with cash, contraband, and flashy trinkets. The mention of Brick and Steel's retirement sparks a heated debate, with Jack passionately arguing against the idea in his native Vietnamese, as subtitled dialogue reveals his fervent emotion. Dan's cool, calculating response - to "retire" their rivals permanently - ignites a conflict that hints at darker intentions beneath the surface of their hedonistic lifestyle, leaving the audience wondering where this conversation will ultimately lead.

scenes.content:
Steel, in the middle of a heated phone call:

STEEL
They're coming out of the woodwork!
(pause)
No, everybody we've put away!
(pause)
Point Blank Sniper?

.SNIPER SCOPE POV

From what seems like only INCHES AWAY.  _Steel's face FILLS the *Leupold Mark 4* scope_.

STEEL
The man's a myth!

Steel turns and looks straight into the cross-hairs.

STEEL
(oh crap)
Hello...

CUT TO:

.OPENING TITLES

> BRICK BRADDOCK <
> & DICK STEEL IN <

> BRICK & STEEL <
> FULL RETIRED <

SMASH CUT TO:

dialogues.text: They're coming out of the woodwork!

scenes.content:
COGNITO, the criminal mastermind, is SLAMMED against the wall.

COGNITO
Woah woah woah, Brick and Steel!

Sure enough, it's Brick and Steel, roughing up their favorite usual suspect.

COGNITO
What is it you want with me, DICK?

Steel SMACKS him.

STEEL
Who's coming after us?

COGNITO
Everyone's coming after you mate!  Scorpio, The Boy Band, Sparrow, Point Blank Sniper...

As he rattles off the long list, Brick and Steel share a look.  This is going to be BAD.

CUT TO:

dialogues.text: Woah woah woah, Brick and Steel!
What is it you want with me, DICK?
Everyone's coming after you mate!  Scorpio, The Boy Band, Sparrow, Point Blank Sniper...

scenes.content:
BRICK and STEEL get into Mom's PORSCHE, Steel at the wheel.  They pause for a beat, the gravity of the situation catching up with them.

BRICK
This is everybody we've ever put away.

STEEL
(starting the engine)
So much for retirement!

They speed off.  To destiny!

CUT TO:

dialogues.text: This is everybody we've ever put away.

scenes.content:
An EXTREMELY HANDSOME MAN drinks a beer.  Shirtless, unfortunately.

His minion approaches offscreen:

MINION
We found Brick and Steel!

HANDSOME MAN
I want them dead.  DEAD!

Beer flies.

> BURN TO PINK.

> THE END <

storySummary:
Retired detective Brick Braddock's idyllic retirement is disrupted by the revelation that his former partner and friend, Dick Steel, shares his growing disillusionment with the idea. As they initially celebrate their newfound freedom on a sun-kissed patio, their jests and camaraderie slowly give way to a tense, unspoken understanding that hints at deeper issues beneath the surface of their comfortable lives.

This newfound unease is mirrored in the Boy Band's trailer home, where Dan and Jack are caught up in a heated debate sparked by Brick and Steel's retirement news. Jack's passionate argument against retirement takes on a more sinister tone as he reveals his true intentions - to eliminate their rivals permanently - rather than simply enjoying their spoils of success. As Dan appears to be more calculating and detached, the tension between them builds, foreshadowing a darker future for these two hedonistic partners.

The convergence of Brick's internal conflict with Jack's external one sets in motion a chain of events that will lead to a dramatic confrontation between the retired detectives and the Boy Band. The seeds of discord sown on Brick's patio are destined to sprout into chaos, as both individuals are drawn into a world where loyalty is tested, and the lines between right and wrong become increasingly blurred. As the story unfolds, it becomes clear that retirement may not be an escape from the past, but rather a catalyst for new conflicts and unforeseen consequences.
